question_text: A light bulb has a resistance of 20Ω. When connected to a 5V power supply, what is the power dissipated by the bulb? (Use P = IV or P = I²R or P = V²/R)
explanation: Using P = V²/R: P = (5V)² ÷ 20Ω = 25 ÷ 20 = 1.25W. Alternatively, find current first (I = 5V ÷ 20Ω = 0.25A) then use P = IV = 0.25A × 5V = 1.25W.
